title: Connelly Early once again gave losing Red Sox a chance to win, but failed to pitch deeper into the game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Connelly Early allowed one run in 4.1 innings but struggled with command, leading to an early exit in the Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Cardinals.
Early recorded five strikeouts despite command issues, contributing to a high pitch count that prevented him from finishing the fifth inning.
With a record of 4-9, the Red Sox's road struggles continue as they prepare for a series-tying game against St. Louis on Saturday.

title: It’s been decades since a center won the NBA Most Improved Player award. Will Neemias Queta be next?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Neemias Queta is a top candidate for the NBA Most Improved Player, last won by a center in 2002.
Queta has played 75 games, started 74, and averages double his previous minutes with 17 double-doubles this season.
Celtics secure No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference, benefiting from Queta's contributions following offseason roster changes.

title: The Red Sox need to shake up the top of their lineup to avoid slow starts
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox lineup struggles with slow starts, highlighted by a weak performance against Dodgers pitcher Dustin May.
Manager Alex Cora considers reshuffling, exploring options like Wilyer Abreu batting fourth and Masataka Yoshida as leadoff.
Prospect Mickey Gasper, hitting .400 in Worcester, is considered for a call-up to boost Boston's lineup.

title: Celtics clinch No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference playoffs with blowout win over Pelicans
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Celtics secured the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference playoffs after defeating the New Orleans Pelicans 144-118.
Boston tied the NBA's single-game record with 29 three-pointers during their victory over the Pelicans at TD Garden.
Jaylen Brown scored 23 points as the Celtics ensured their 12th consecutive playoff appearance, the NBA's longest active streak.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Pizza Movie" starring Gaten Matarazzo and Sean Giambrone is now streaming on Hulu.
"The Boys" Season 5, a dark superhero series, is streaming on Prime Video.
"Sirat," an Oscar-nominated film about a search in Morocco, is available on Hulu.

title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum won't play against the Pelicans due to right Achilles management before the NBA playoffs.
Jaylen Brown, managing left Achilles tendinitis, will be available against the Pelicans despite being listed as questionable.
Celtics seek to solidify No. 2 seed in Eastern Conference with a win or a Knicks loss against the Raptors.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's cancer battle.
Her daughter Emma was diagnosed with medulloblastoma in April 2025, undergoing surgery and extensive treatment at MGH.
Robertson will complete the marathon to support Emma's bravery and future, while acknowledging the MGH medical team.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens won't debut for the Bruins against Tampa Bay, as announced by coach Marco Sturm.
Hagens, a 19-year-old prospect, needs time to adjust before playing amid Boston's playoff push.
Coach Sturm emphasizes that Hagens must earn his spot and will decide his debut timing carefully.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes ranks 250 'Greatest Self-Made Americans,' including Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers.
Historical figures with Massachusetts ties on Forbes list: Benjamin Franklin, Sheldon Adelson, and Red Sox legends Babe Ruth, Ted Williams.
Noubar Afeyan ranked No. 27; founder of Flagship Pioneering and chairman of Moderna with estimated net worth of $1.9 billion.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon takes place on April 20, 2026, starting in Hopkinton and ending at Copley Square.
This year features 30,000 runners from over 130 countries, with six starting waves increasing from four.
Boston Marathon can be watched live on WCVB Channel 5 and streamed through the Very Local app.
